rules.rule: Every team and project must document its business nature — what the business is.
Every team and project must document its business goals — the desired outcomes the business pursues.
Every team and project must document its future considerations — growth plans and expansion scenarios.
All three purpose elements (nature, goals, future considerations) must be present before engineering work begins.
Scenarios must include an actor (Given), an interaction (When), and an outcome (Then).
Scenarios must be written in non-technical language unless the business itself is technical in nature.
Scenario outcomes must be expressible as marketing copy understandable by non-technical stakeholders.
Scenarios may chain — each subsequent scenario may build on the established context of prior scenarios.
Scenarios must represent the perspective of end users or customers — not developers or system components.
Multiple scenarios should be written to cover all primary actors and major use cases of the system.
